'''Cliff Beverley''' (born 25 March 1977) is a New Zealand former professional [[rugby league]] footballer. His preferred position was {{rlp|fe}}, although he was somewhat of a utility and spent considerable time at {{rlp|fb}}.

==Background==
Beverley was born in New Zealand.

==Early years==
A junior from the [[Glenora Bears|Glenora club]], his childhood hero was [[Wally Lewis]].<ref>[http://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby_league/s/115/115270_its_kids_stuff_for_beverley.html It's kids' stuff for Beverley] ''Manchester Evening New'', 27 April 2004</ref> Beverley played in the [[1995 Lion Red Cup]] for the [[Waitakere City Raiders]].<ref>''Lion Red Rugby League Annual 1995'', [[New Zealand Rugby Football League]], 1995. p.106</ref> He also represented [[Auckland rugby league team|Auckland]] in the [[1998 New Zealand rugby league season|1998 domestic season]] and Auckland North in 1999.<ref name=p125>''New Zealand Rugby League Annual '98'', [[New Zealand Rugby Football League]], 1998. p.125</ref><ref name=hoppe>{{cite web|url=http://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/news/article.cfm?c_id=4&objectid=3727|title=Rugby League: Injury gives Hoppe a starting chance|author=Jessup, Peter|date=16 March 1999|work=[[The New Zealand Herald]]|accessdate=24 September 2011}}</ref>

==Warriors==
Beverley signed for the then [[Auckland Warriors]] in 1999. He made his début in round four, replacing an injured [[Matthew Ridge]].<ref>[http://www.rugbyleague.co.nz/player.aspx?id=218&teamID=4 Cliff Beverley] ''rugbyleague.co.nz''</ref> He played 44 [[National Rugby League]] games for the club, half of them from the bench.{{citation needed|date=May 2022}}

==England==
In 2001 Beverley moved to England to play for the [[Barrow Raiders]] in the [[RFL League 1|National Leagues]]. He then transferred to the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] in 2002. Here he topped the try-scoring list, scoring 38 tries, and helped the club win the [[RFL Championship|National League One]] Grand Final and promotion to the [[Super League]].<ref>[http://www.rleague.com/db/article.php?id=18347 Leading Scorers in Britain after Round 27] {{webarchive|url=https://archive.today/20120913165030/http://www.rleague.com/db/article.php?id=18347 |date=2012-09-13}} ''rleague.com'', 17 Sep 2003</ref> After receiving interest from several Super League clubs including [[Hull F.C.]] and [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] he decided to remain at Salford City Reds to help them succeed in the top division.<ref>[http://www.salfordadvertiser.co.uk/sport/s/437259_bevs_ready_for_a_2_year_deal Bev's ready for a 2 year deal] ''Salford Advertiser'', 17/7/2003</ref> He remained with the club for two more years, playing 48 Super League games.

==Later years==
Beverley returned to New Zealand at the end of the 2005 season citing home sickness.<ref>[http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/salford/4703043.stm Beverley poised for Salford exit] ''BBC Sport'', 21 July 2005</ref> He then joined the new [[Waitakere Rangers]] franchise in the [[Bartercard Cup]] before retiring at the end of 2007.<ref>[http://www.leagueunlimited.com/article.php?newsid=14398 Bartercard Cup - Round 14 team lists] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110928082929/http://www.leagueunlimited.com/article.php?newsid=14398|date=28 September 2011}} ''NZRL Media Release'', 19 July 2007</ref>
